what_it_does: Defines a small React component TopBar({notifications=7}) that renders a top-right toolbar containing a bell icon (with a count badge shown when notifications > 0) and an avatar icon, both as inline SVGs. Attaches it to window.TopBar.
observations: Single prop `notifications` defaulting to 7; the badge renders conditionally only when the count is positive.
Bell and avatar are inline SVG paths (no external icon dependency).
Uses cv-* classes (cv-topbar, cv-bell, cv-bell-badge, cv-avatar) for styling — no inline style block here.
Accessibility: bell span has role='button' and aria-label='Notifications'; avatar has none.
standouts: Purely class-driven styling (no inline tokens), unusual among sibling kit files that lean on inline var() styles.
conventions: Module-as-window-global export, consistent with the kit's babel-script load model.
Uses cv-* design-system classes rather than inline styles, leaning on the stylesheet for the look.
Inline SVG icons rather than an icon font/library.
concerns: Avatar lacks role/aria-label that the bell has — minor accessibility inconsistency.
Default notifications=7 is a hardcoded mock value (expected for a design kit).
notes: A tiny presentational top-right bar (bell-with-badge + avatar) styled entirely via cv-* classes, exported on window.TopBar.
questions: Is the notifications count wired to any real source, or always the mock default in this kit?
Should the avatar be interactive (it has no role/handler here)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the comment 'bell + avatar pinned top-right of content column' is exactly what it renders.
intention_signals: Provides the reusable top-bar chrome (notifications + identity) that sits atop each content screen in the platform kit.
contribution: Contributes the top-bar chrome pattern — a notification bell with conditional count badge plus an avatar — as a small reusable, class-styled header element.
summary_for_embedding: A React presentational TopBar component rendering a notification bell with a conditional count badge and an avatar as inline SVGs, exported on window.TopBar.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the reusable top-right chrome (notifications and identity) for the platform kit's content screens.
